title: Section — Budget (per-trade lines + editable rollup)
description: Two views of the budget, both in CSI MasterFormat order. (1) lines: detailed per-trade budget lines — each references a QTO takeoff_id so its quantity is PULLED from the Summary QTO (the single source of truth), not restated; the workbook renders these as one page per trade with live qty links, unit costs, and a trade total. (2) rollup: the high-level editable budget — one row per top-level cost code / trade with an estimated value (from the trade pages) that a user can override or replace with a selected bid, and a running project total. The model supplies structure and any extracted numbers; it does not invent pricing.

trade_id.description: Trade page this line belongs to (trades.trades[].trade_id).

cost_code.description: Sub-division split within the trade (demo | new_work | schedule | ...).

qto_takeoff_id.description: QTO line this quantity is pulled from (quantity-takeoff.items[].takeoff_id). The workbook links the cell to the QTO so editing the QTO updates the budget.

quantity.description: Mirror of the QTO quantity for offline readers; the live workbook value comes from the QTO link.

unit_cost.description: Estimated cost per unit. Null until an estimating tool/user fills it.

extended_cost.description: quantity x unit_cost; computed by the workbook formula when both are present.

rollup.description: The high-level editable budget: one row per top-level trade/cost code. estimated_value comes from the trade pages; selected_bid_id lets a user swap in a submitted bid; override lets them set a manual number. The workbook keeps a running project total.

estimated_value.description: Rolled up from the trade's budget page.

selected_bid_id.description: bid-log.bids[].bid_id the user chose to use instead of the estimate.

manual_override.description: User-entered value that wins over estimate/selected bid when set.
